findings.evidence: Lines 80-83 prove `h ∈ I.comap f hf ↔ f h ∈ I`; lines 121-130 and 152-154 use one shared `hf` in theorem statements, so the current API unnecessarily depends on choosing the same proof witness.
findings.fix: State these with independent witnesses, e.g. `(⊥).comap f hf₁ = ker f hf₂`, `(I ⊔ J).comap f hf₀ = I.comap f hf₁ ⊔ J.comap f hf₂`, and for `iSup` use a separate `hf₀` on the left and `hfᵢ : ∀ i, Function.Surjective f` on the right.
findings.issue: `comap_bot`, `comap_sup_of_surjective`, `comap_iSup_of_surjective`, and `comap_sSup_of_surjective` only rewrite when all comaps/kernels are built with the same surjectivity witness, even though the carrier is independent of that proof.

summary: The construction is at the intended surjective level, but part of the API still treats surjectivity proofs as mathematical data. Generalize the remaining comparison/lattice lemmas so callers are not forced to use the same witness everywhere.
